stem: The line segment \( y = [[m]]x + [[cc]] \), for \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \), is revolved about the \( x \)-axis. Find the area of the surface generated.
distractor_rationales: This omits the slant factor \( \sqrt{1+(f')^{2}} \).
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This drops the factor of \( 2 \) in \( 2\pi f\sqrt{1+(f')^{2}} \).
This doubles the correct surface area.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct surface area.

stem: The arc of the circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}=[[rsq]] \) given by \( y=\sqrt{[[rsq]]-x^{2}} \), for \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \), is revolved about the \( x \)-axis, forming a band (zone) on a sphere of radius \( [[r]] \). Find the surface area of this band.
solution: For a sphere, the surface-of-revolution integrand \( 2\pi y\sqrt{1+(y')^{2}} \) simplifies to the CONSTANT \( 2\pi [[r]] \) (Archimedes' hat-box theorem: a zone's area depends only on its height, not its position). So the area is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi[[r]]\,dx = 2\pi[[r]]([[b]]-[[a]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the surface area of the WHOLE sphere, not just this band.
This mistakenly reintroduces an \( x \)-dependence that canceled out of the integrand.
This treats the band as a flat annulus with no geometric basis.
This doubles the correct surface area.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct surface area.

stem: The line segment \( x = [[m]]y + [[c]] \), for \( [[p]] \le y \le [[q]] \), is revolved about the \( y \)-axis. Find the area of the surface generated.
solution: With \( g(y)=[[m]]y+[[c]] \) and \( g'(y)=[[m]] \), revolving about the \( y \)-axis gives surface area \( \int_{[[p]]}^{[[q]]} 2\pi g(y)\sqrt{1+(g'(y))^{2}}\,dy = 2\pi\sqrt{1+[[m]]^{2}}\int_{[[p]]}^{[[q]]}([[m]]y+[[c]])\,dy = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the slant factor \( \sqrt{1+(g')^{2}} \).
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This drops the factor of \( 2 \) in \( 2\pi g\sqrt{1+(g')^{2}} \).
This doubles the correct surface area.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct surface area.

stem: The curve \( y = \cosh(x) \), for \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \), is revolved about the \( x \)-axis, forming a catenoid. Find the area of the surface generated.
solution: Here \( y'=\sinh(x) \), so \( \sqrt{1+(y')^{2}} = \sqrt{1+\sinh^{2}(x)} = \cosh(x) \). The surface area is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi\cosh(x)\cdot\cosh(x)\,dx = \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi\cosh^{2}(x)\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( 2\pi\int\cosh(x)\,dx \) (the arc-length-style integral), forgetting the extra factor of \( y=\cosh(x) \) itself.
This halves the correct surface area.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This doubles the correct surface area.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct surface area.
